purpose: Define the minimum Firebase Auth custom claims needed to evaluate the Phase A Firestore DEV/staging rules draft without activating real Auth or runtime backend.

tenantId.description: Tenant isolation claim. For TyA this must resolve to the TyA tenant id used in Firestore document paths.

role.description: Canonical role expected for Phase A access decisions.

projectId.description: Primary project scope for users limited to one project.

projectIds.description: Project scope list for admin, ops, shopper or cliente users with access to more than one project.

shopperId.description: Stable shopper identifier used for self-scope reads and future shopper-owned writes.

roleAliases.reason: The current Firestore DEV/staging rules draft references coordinador, while the Phase A role model also uses ops. This must be normalized before real Auth claims are issued.

transitionalCompatibility.projectScopeFallback: projectId may be accepted for single-project users; projectIds should be preferred for multi-project and SaaS behavior.

super.notes: Super can administer tenants but must still avoid production use until authorized.

admin.notes: Admin manages Phase A project operations.

ops.notes: Ops/coordinator role for operational management after role naming is locked.

shopper.notes: Shopper access must remain self-scoped where documents are shopper-specific.

cliente.notes: Cliente read scope must be reviewed before real DEV/staging Auth.

hardStops: Do not activate real Firebase Auth from this contract.
Do not deploy Firestore rules from this contract.
Do not create real users, tokens or claims from this contract.
Do not write Firestore data from this contract.
Do not connect Make, Gemini, WhatsApp or Storage real integrations from this contract.
readinessDecisionsRequiredBeforeDevAuth: Lock canonical operational role name: ops versus coordinador.
Confirm whether admin and super may bypass projectIds or must also carry explicit project scope.
Confirm cliente read scope by collection before emulator validation.
Confirm shopper write permissions for postulations, agenda, visit completion and questionnaire status in the future action contract.
Confirm final sensitive-field policy for contact, payment and NDA-related attributes.
